gene_description: HMG-CoA Reductase — rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol biosynthesis and the direct target of statin drugs. Variants in HMGCR affect enzyme expression and statin-induced cholesterol lowering efficacy.

source.title: A paucimorphic variant in the HMG-CoA reductase gene is associated with lipid-lowering response to statin treatment in diabetes: a GoDARTS study
source.finding: HMGCR rs17238540 G allele carriers show 22-23% reduced statin efficacy for total cholesterol and triglyceride lowering. G allele frequencies vary by population; TT homozygotes show 28% treatment failure vs 51% in G allele carriers.

TT.phenotype: Normal statin response / strong cholesterol reduction
TT.effect: Homozygous for reference allele. Standard HMG-CoA reductase expression. Optimal statin efficacy. Mean total cholesterol reduction of 41.8 mg/dL with pravastatin. 28% fail to reach cholesterol targets. Represents ~95.7% of Europeans.
TT.recommendation: Standard statin dosing and monitoring. Expect good cholesterol reduction. Assess response at 4-6 weeks and titrate as needed per lipid guidelines.

TG.phenotype: Reduced statin response / moderate cholesterol reduction
TG.effect: Heterozygous G carrier. Modestly reduced HMG-CoA reductase expression or activity. Approximately 22-23% reduced statin efficacy compared to TT. Mean total cholesterol reduction of 32.5 mg/dL with pravastatin. Higher failure rates to reach targets (51% in some cohorts).
TG.recommendation: May require higher statin doses to achieve target LDL-C. Intensify lipid monitoring at 4-6 weeks. Consider combination therapy (ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitors) if targets not met with standard dosing.

GT.phenotype: Reduced statin response / moderate cholesterol reduction
GT.effect: Heterozygous G carrier. Modestly reduced HMG-CoA reductase expression or activity. Approximately 22-23% reduced statin efficacy compared to TT. Mean total cholesterol reduction of 32.5 mg/dL with pravastatin. Higher failure rates to reach targets (51% in some cohorts).
GT.recommendation: May require higher statin doses to achieve target LDL-C. Intensify lipid monitoring at 4-6 weeks. Consider combination therapy (ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitors) if targets not met with standard dosing.

GG.effect: Homozygous for minor allele (rare, ~0.06% in Europeans). Marked reduction in statin efficacy. Substantially impaired cholesterol lowering response. Highest failure rates for reaching lipid targets.
GG.recommendation: Likely to require higher statin doses or combination lipid-lowering therapy. Early intensive monitoring essential. Consider PCSK9 inhibitors or ezetimibe addition. Genetic counseling regarding familial hypercholesterolemia risk.
